compiled_state.title: GREEN: Add config-driven import patterns and update count_centrality()
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Add per-language import pattern configuration to dso-config.conf and update count_centrality() in record-test-status.sh to read patterns from config instead of using hardcoded grep patterns.

### Implementation steps

1. **Add commented-out config keys to .claude/dso-config.conf** (following existing convention of commenting optional keys):
   - # test_gate.import_pattern.python=import $MODULE|from $MODULE import
   - # test_gate.import_pattern.flask=from flask import|from $MODULE import
   - # test_gate.import_pattern.ruby=require '$MODULE'|require_relative '$MODULE'
   - # test_gate.import_pattern.typescript=import .* from '$MODULE'|require\('$MODULE'\)
   - # test_gate.import_pattern.java=import .*\.$MODULE
   - # test_gate.import_pattern.bash=source .*$MODULE|\. .*$MODULE

2. **Update count_centrality() in plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh**:
   a. Read all test_gate.import_pattern.* keys from config via grep
   b. Skip entries with empty values
   c. For each pattern: replace literal $MODULE with the escaped module name (reuse existing sed escaping logic)
   d. Validate each converted pattern: run echo '' | grep -E "<pattern>" /dev/null 2>/dev/null; if exit code != 0 and != 1, emit stderr warning "WARNING: invalid import pattern '<key>': <pattern> — skipping" and exclude from combined pattern
   e. Build combined grep pattern from all valid configured patterns joined with |
   f. When no valid config patterns exist (none configured, all empty, or all invalid), fall back to current hardcoded patterns
   g. Use the combined pattern in the existing grep -rlE invocation

3. **Remove RED marker from .test-index** after all tests pass

### File Impact
- .claude/dso-config.conf (modify — add 6 commented import pattern keys)
- plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh (modify — update count_centrality() function)
- .test-index (modify — remove RED marker)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py 2>/dev/null || true
- [ ] .claude/dso-config.conf contains 6 commented import pattern keys (python, flask, ruby, typescript, java, bash)
  Verify: grep -c '^# test_gate\.import_pattern\.' .claude/dso-config.conf | awk '{exit ($1 < 6)}'
- [ ] count_centrality() reads test_gate.import_pattern.* keys from config
  Verify: grep -q 'test_gate\.import_pattern' plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh
- [ ] Invalid patterns produce stderr warning and are skipped
  Verify: grep -q 'invalid import pattern' plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh
- [ ] Fallback to hardcoded patterns when no config patterns exist
  Verify: grep -q 'fallback\|default.*pattern\|hardcoded' plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh
- [ ] RED marker removed from .test-index
  Verify: ! grep -q '\[test_config_pattern_used_for_centrality\]' .test-index
- [ ] All centrality tests pass (GREEN)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 2/6: Code patterns understood ✓ — count_centrality() uses hardcoded grep pattern; need to add config-driven test_gate.import_pattern.* key support with fallback to hardcoded defaults
comments.author: Test
